domains.name: Finance
domains.description: Revenue, billing, and unit-economics definitions: MRR, ARR, churn, CAC, and revenue recognition rules.

domains.name: Engineering
domains.description: API contracts, performance SLAs, deployment process, and reliability/error-budget conventions.

domains.name: Product
domains.description: Product analytics and engagement metrics: NPS, adoption, trial conversion, DAU/MAU.

domains.name: Data
domains.description: Warehouse schemas, ETL scheduling, PII handling, and data-retention/soft-delete patterns.

domains.name: Operations
domains.description: Incident severity, customer health scoring, and support SLAs.

domains.name: Infrastructure
domains.description: Auto-scaling, backups, disaster recovery, and environment-refresh conventions.

items.title: Monthly Recurring Revenue (MRR) includes only
items.content: Monthly Recurring Revenue (MRR) includes only recurring subscription charges. One-time fees, usage overages, and professional services revenue are excluded. MRR = SUM(active_subscriptions.monthly_amount) where subscription.status = 'active'.

items.title: Our official churn metric is MRR
items.content: Our official churn metric is MRR churn, not logo churn. Churn Rate = MRR lost from cancelled/downgraded subscriptions / Total MRR at period start. A customer downgrading from Enterprise to Starter counts as partial churn.

items.title: Annual Recurring Revenue (ARR) = MRR
items.content: Annual Recurring Revenue (ARR) = MRR * 12, but only for customers on annual or multi-year contracts. Month-to-month customers are excluded from ARR reporting to investors.

items.title: Revenue is recognized ratably over the
items.content: Revenue is recognized ratably over the contract period per ASC 606. Upfront payments create deferred revenue. Professional services revenue is recognized upon delivery milestone completion.

items.title: Customer Acquisition Cost (CAC) = (Sales
items.content: Customer Acquisition Cost (CAC) = (Sales + Marketing spend) / New customers acquired. Excludes organic/referral customers. Payback period target is under 18 months.

items.title: Net Promoter Score is calculated on
items.content: Net Promoter Score is calculated on a rolling 90-day window of survey responses. Only responses from active customers are included. Target NPS is 50+. Detractors (0-6), Passives (7-8), Promoters (9-10).

items.title: A feature is considered 'adopted' by
items.content: A feature is considered 'adopted' by a user when they use it on 3+ distinct days within a 7-day window. Single usage events count as 'tried' not 'adopted'.

items.title: Free trial lasts 14 days. Conversion
items.content: Free trial lasts 14 days. Conversion is attributed to the trial if payment occurs within 7 days after trial expiry. After 7 days post-expiry, it counts as a re-engagement conversion.

items.title: Daily Active Users / Monthly Active
items.content: Daily Active Users / Monthly Active Users ratio measures stickiness. Our target is 40% for the core product. Mobile app DAU/MAU is tracked separately. An 'active' user must perform a meaningful action (not just login).

items.title: Public API is rate-limited at 1000
items.content: Public API is rate-limited at 1000 requests per minute per tenant API key. Burst allowance is 50 requests. Rate limit headers (X-RateLimit-Remaining, X-RateLimit-Reset) are included in every response.

items.title: All user-facing database queries must complete
items.content: All user-facing database queries must complete within 500ms at p95. Queries exceeding this threshold trigger an alert in Datadog. Background/batch queries have a 30-second timeout.

items.title: Production deployments require at least two
items.content: Production deployments require at least two code review approvals. Hotfixes can proceed with one approval from a senior engineer plus post-deploy review within 24 hours.

items.title: Our SLA guarantees 99.9% uptime (43.8
items.content: Our SLA guarantees 99.9% uptime (43.8 minutes downtime/month). Error budget is tracked weekly. When budget is exhausted, feature releases are frozen until reliability improves.

items.title: The orders table uses order_id (UUID)
items.content: The orders table uses order_id (UUID) as primary key. Each row represents a single order event. Amendments create new rows with same customer_id and a parent_order_id reference. Status enum: draft, confirmed, fulfilled, cancelled, refunded.

items.title: Main ETL pipeline is scheduled at
items.content: Main ETL pipeline is scheduled at 03:00 UTC. Data lands in the warehouse by 04:30 UTC. Downstream dashboards refresh at 05:00 UTC. If pipeline fails, on-call is paged after 30-minute retry window.

items.title: All PII columns (email, phone, address
items.content: All PII columns (email, phone, address, SSN) must be SHA-256 hashed in the analytics/reporting layer. Raw PII is only accessible in the raw/staging layer with explicit IAM permissions. Hashing uses a project-wide salt stored in Secret Manager.

items.title: All business entities use soft-delete (deleted_at
items.content: All business entities use soft-delete (deleted_at timestamp). Hard deletes are only for GDPR erasure requests. Soft-deleted records are excluded from analytics views but retained in raw tables for 7 years.

items.title: S1: Full outage, all hands on
items.content: S1: Full outage, all hands on deck, 15-min response. S2: Major feature broken, 30-min response. S3: Minor degradation, next business day. S4: Cosmetic/low-impact, scheduled sprint work. S1/S2 require post-mortems within 48 hours.

items.title: Health Score (0-100) = 0.3 *
items.content: Health Score (0-100) = 0.3 * usage_score + 0.25 * support_score + 0.2 * engagement_score + 0.15 * payment_score + 0.1 * growth_score. Accounts below 40 are flagged for CSM intervention. Recalculated weekly.

items.title: Tier 1 tickets: 4-hour first response
items.content: Tier 1 tickets: 4-hour first response, 24-hour resolution target. Tier 2: 2-hour first response, 8-hour resolution. Enterprise/S1: 30-minute first response, 4-hour resolution. SLA compliance target is 95%.

items.title: Kubernetes HPA scales up when average
items.content: Kubernetes HPA scales up when average CPU exceeds 70% for 3 minutes. Scale-down happens at 30% CPU sustained for 10 minutes. Min replicas: 3, Max replicas: 50. Memory-based scaling triggers at 80%.

items.title: Database backups: daily snapshots retained for
items.content: Database backups: daily snapshots retained for 30 days, weekly snapshots retained for 1 year. Point-in-time recovery available for last 7 days. Backup integrity verified monthly via restore test.

items.title: Staging database is refreshed every Monday
items.content: Staging database is refreshed every Monday at 02:00 UTC from a sanitized production snapshot. PII is anonymized during refresh. Staging SSL certs are managed separately from production.

items.title: When calculating gross margin, cloud provider
items.content: When calculating gross margin, cloud provider credits (AWS/GCP) should be excluded from COGS. This gives a more accurate picture of sustainable unit economics. Credits are temporary and distort margins.

items.title: User sessions should timeout after 30
items.content: User sessions should timeout after 30 minutes of inactivity. This balances security with usability. Refresh tokens extend active sessions. OAuth sessions follow the IdP's timeout.

items.title: Proposing a new 'Weekly Active Teams'
items.content: Proposing a new 'Weekly Active Teams' metric: a team is 'active' if 2+ members performed a meaningful action in the last 7 days. This better captures B2B engagement than individual DAU.
